intro: Triquiliopsis palmeri, commonly known as Cat's Claw, is a woody vine native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it beneficial in the treatment of digestive issues, anxiety, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cat's Claw include alkaloids such as oxindole and isoxindole alkaloids, as well as glycosides and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Cat's Claw is commonly used to make teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being, support digestive health, and reduce inflammation.
